human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for aerospace quality-system specificity, source quality, hold/traceability boundaries, supplier escalation posture, deployment and commercialization completeness, and explicit specification-based runtime positioning.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to aerospace quality-system evidence review, hold/release enforcement, traceability conflict handling, supplier escalation, repeat-defect and incident packaging.
Authority sources cover FAA production guidance, eCFR Part 21, and recognized aerospace quality standards with explicit refresh policies and source hierarchy.

Evaluation scenarios cover in-scope reviews, hold/release boundary enforcement, contradictory evidence, incident escalation, supplier substitution, close-without-review, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/aerospace-and-defense/quality-systems-aerospace-specialist/ so aerospace quality work is kept distinct from adjacent engineering, planning, and legal/compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong across release overrides, substitution, formal approval ownership, and safety-critical disposition.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated aerospace quality-systems specialist that supports bounded evidence-backed hold/release, CAPA, and supplier-quality workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based and does not claim retained-implementation authority until a separate promotion decision is made.
